# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd service mesh configuration for Kubernetes. Set up linkerd.io/inject annotation for automatic proxy injection. Configure server and authorization policies for services. This is for our microservices communication security.
# Category: linkerd_no_mtls

apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: my-microservices-namespace # Replace with your actual namespace name
  annotations:
    linkerd.io/inject: enabled # Enables automatic Linkerd proxy injection for this namespace
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: my-service-server # A unique name for this server policy
  namespace: my-microservices-namespace # Must be in the same namespace as the target pods
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-service # Selects pods with the label 'app: my-service'
  port: 8080 # The port on the pod that this server policy applies to (e.g., your microservice's API port)
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/1 # Or HTTP/2, GRPC, or Opaque. Use HTTP/1 for typical REST APIs.
  # clientAuth: requireMutualTLS # Uncomment to require mTLS for connections to this server (recommended for security)
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: MeshTLSAuthentication
metadata:
  name: internal-microservices-auth # A unique name for this authentication policy
  namespace: my-microservices-namespace
spec:
  identities:
    # List the Linkerd mTLS identities of authorized client services.
    # Format: <service-account-name>.<namespace>.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local
    - client-service-a.my-microservices-namespace.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local
    - client-service-b.my-microservices-namespace.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local
    # You can also specify identities from other namespaces
    - another-client.another-namespace.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local
  # Alternatively, use serviceAccountSelector to select service accounts by label:
  # serviceAccountSelector:
  #   matchLabels:
  #     security-group: internal-clients
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-internal-access-to-my-service # A unique name for this authorization policy
  namespace: my-microservices-namespace
spec:
  targetRef:
    group: policy.linkerd.io
    kind: Server
    name: my-service-server # References the Server resource defined above
  requiredAuthenticationRef:
    group: policy.linkerd.io
    kind: MeshTLSAuthentication
    name: internal-microservices-auth # References the MeshTLSAuthentication resource defined above
